phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your packmate dive bursts beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — packmate dive bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your packmate dive bursts out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your packmate dive bursts beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your packmate dive bursts on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your packmate dive bursts in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Anivia's wall-frost poke control own this window — your packmate dive bursts don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-Q the bleed-consume on his last hits — the double dagger chunks harder than the lobby expects at one.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Pack pressure begins: hounds chase what you Q — trade where the dogs can pile on.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: W online: the lock-on dive threatens anyone past the wave's midline. The lane plays scared now.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your packmate dive bursts out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your packmate dive bursts beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Your packmate dive bursts out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control on first item — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Naafiri flanks: R the sweep, W the carry, and let the pack vote on the peel.

phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Yone's double-blade spirit dives edge out your packmate dive bursts on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Naafiri flanks: R the sweep, W the carry, and let the pack vote on the peel.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-Q the bleed-consume on his last hits — the double dagger chunks harder than the lobby expects at one.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Pack pressure begins: hounds chase what you Q — trade where the dogs can pile on.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: W online: the lock-on dive threatens anyone past the wave's midline. The lane plays scared now.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Q-E strings on every contact — the pack heals you through trades and the bleed math compounds.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: R online: the vision sweep plus bigger pack makes every roam a scheduled accident for a side lane.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: the W dive plus full string deletes squishies. Hunt the half-bars.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Naafiri flanks: R the sweep, W the carry, and let the pack vote on the peel.
